popularStory.headline: Richard York II
popularStory.description: Richard York II (27 Nov 1650 – 6 Mar 1695)  was born on November 27, 1650 in Olney, Buckinghamshire, England.  He is the son of Richard York I (22 Oct 1620 - 1660) of Naseby, Northamptonshire, Englan …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard York II於1650年11月27日出生在英國巴金哈姆爾時，他的父親Richard Yorke30歲，母親Mary Jane Seymour28歲。他於1682年11月14日與Ann Seymour在英國巴金哈姆爾奧爾尼結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1695年3月6日在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾米爾頓 凱恩斯享年44歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾米爾頓 凱恩斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard York II于1650年11月27日出生在英国巴金哈姆爾时，他的父亲Richard Yorke30岁，母亲Mary Jane Seymour28岁。他于1682年11月14日与Ann Seymour在英国巴金哈姆爾奧爾尼结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1695年3月6日在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾米爾頓 凱恩斯享年44岁，埋葬在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾米爾頓 凱恩斯。

nameMeanings.name: York
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of York in northern England. The surname is now widespread throughout England. Originally, the city bore the Latin name Eburacum, which is probably from a Brittonic name meaning ‘yew-tree place’. This was altered by folk etymology to Old English Eoforwīc (from the elements eofor ‘wild boar’ + wīc ‘specialized farmstead’). This name was taken over by Scandinavian settlers, who altered it back to opacity in the form Jórvík or Jórk (English York, which became finally settled as the placename in the 13th century). The surname has also been adopted by Jews as an Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames.
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of the East Slavic patronymic   Yurkovich   or its Croatian, Slovak, or Slovenian variants. Compare   Yurk  .

variants.name: Yurkovich
meanings: Ukrainian (standard transliteration Yurkovych) and Belorussian: patronymic from the personal name Yurko and its Belorussian equivalent Yurka, pet forms of Ukrainian Yuriy and Belorussian Yuray, equivalents of Greek Geōrgios (see George ).
Americanized form of Croatian Jurković, Slovak and Slovenian Jurkovič (see Jurkovic , compare Jurkovich and Yurkovic ).
Jewish (from Bessarabia): habitational name for someone from Yurkovtsy in Ukraine.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard York II was born on 27 November 1650, in Buckinghamshire, England, his father, Richard Yorke, was 30 and his mother, Mary Jane Seymour, was 28. He married Ann Seymour on 14 November 1682, in Olney, Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 6 March 1695, in Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 44, and was buried in Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard York II est né le 27 novembre 1650, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Yorke, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Mary Jane Seymour, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Ann Seymour le 14 novembre 1682, à Olney, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 6 mars 1695, à Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

November 1682 in Olney, Buckinghamshire, England Ann Seymour geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 6. März 1695 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard York IIが1650年11月27日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父Richard Yorkeは30歳，母Mary Jane Seymourは28歳であった。1682年11月14日にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアオルニーでAnn Seymourと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1695年3月6日にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアミルトン ケインスで44歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアミルトン ケインスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard York II(이)가 1650년 11월 27일 영국 벅잉함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Yorke(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Jane Seymour(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1682년 11월 14일 영국 벅잉함실르 니에서 Ann Seymour(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1695년 3월 6일 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 미턴 케인스에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 미턴 케인스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard York II nasceu em 27 de novembro de 1650, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Yorke, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Mary Jane Seymour, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Ann Seymour em 14 de novembro de 1682, em Olney, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 6 de março de 1695, em Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em Milton Keynes, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard York II родился 27 ноября 1650 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Yorke было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Jane Seymour, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Ann Seymour 14 ноября 1682 г. в местности олни, буккингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 6 марта 1695 г. в местности милтон кинесь, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности милтон кинесь, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.
